ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for 'Pizzotto' specifically. FOODON_00002572 (pizza food product) is the closest defensible parent category. Pizzotto is a hybrid dish combining pizza and risotto elements.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) is the closest parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Pizzotto as a hybrid pizza-risotto dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Pizzotto or a sufficiently close pizza-risotto hybrid food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual Pizzotto as served at Ricciotti restaurant, estimated at approximately 350 g including dough base, tomato sauce, mozzarella, bacon, mushrooms, and egg.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat and vegetables, regular crust (SR Legacy ID 21299) and component ingredient data
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct USDA or HPB entry exists for 'Pizzotto'. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition modelling using USDA SR Legacy data for: pizza with meat and vegetables on regular crust (FDC ID 21299), mozzarella cheese (FDC ID 1008), bacon (FDC ID 10123), mushrooms raw (FDC ID 169251), whole egg (FDC ID 748967), and tomato sauce (FDC ID 11549). Proportions estimated from typical Italian-style pizza composition with bacon, mushroom, egg, tomato sauce, and mozzarella toppings on a regular wheat dough base. Values represent a plausible per-100g estimate for this dish category.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pizza, cheese, regular crust (GI 57)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pizzotto. GI of 57 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for regular-crust cheese pizza (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), which is the closest analogous food with an approved GI measurement. Pizzotto uses a similar wheat-dough base with cheese and toppings, making this the most defensible analogous GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 95.2) / 100 = 54.2, where carbohydrate per 350g serving = 27.2 × 3.5 = 95.2g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from bacon, mozzarella, and tomato sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Pizzotto due to its high sodium content of approximately 620 mg per 100g (approximately 2170 mg per serving), which substantially contributes to the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg and may elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and high glycemic load per serving from refined wheat dough base
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Pizzotto cautiously, as its medium GI of 57 and high glycemic load of approximately 54 per serving from the refined wheat dough base can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods or fibre-rich vegetables is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from bacon and mozzarella cheese, and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Pizzotto intake due to its saturated fat content of approximately 4.6g per 100g from bacon and mozzarella cheese, combined with high sodium levels, both of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pizzotto is a signature Italian fusion dish served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore, combining elements of traditional pizza and risotto into a single baked preparation. The dish features a pizza-style wheat dough base topped with rich tomato sauce, melted mozzarella cheese, crispy bacon, sautéed mushrooms, and a baked egg, creating a hearty and indulgent meal. The name 'Pizzotto' is a portmanteau of 'pizza' and 'risotto', reflecting its hybrid culinary identity. The texture combines the crispness of a baked pizza crust with the richness of melted cheese and the umami depth of bacon and mushrooms, while the egg adds creaminess and additional protein. Nutritionally, Pizzotto is a calorie-dense dish providing approximately 248 kcal per 100g, with moderate protein (11.5g), significant fat (10.8g including 4.6g saturated), and refined carbohydrates (27.2g) from the wheat dough base. Its sodium content is notably high at approximately 620 mg per 100g, primarily from bacon, mozzarella, and tomato sauce, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The medium glycemic index of 57 and high glycemic load per serving require portion awareness for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Pizzotto is classified under Italian cuisine and is available in Singapore through field survey at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Pizzotto?
What are the ingredients in Pizzotto?
What is the difference between pizza and Pizzotto?
Where can I order Pizzotto in Singapore?
What is the GI of Pizzotto?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Pizzotto exists in any approved primary or secondary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition modelling from USDA SR Legacy data for analogous pizza with meat and vegetable toppings on regular crust, supplemented by individual ingredient data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved GI source (regular-crust cheese pizza, GI 57, University of Sydney GI Database). Confidence is medium rather than low because the dish composition is well-defined from the menu description and the analogous USDA pizza entry is a strong match.
notes: Pizzotto is a proprietary dish name used by Ricciotti restaurant group in Singapore. It is not a traditional Italian dish category and does not appear in standard food composition databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for pizza with meat and vegetables on regular crust (FDC ID 21299) as the primary analogue, with adjustments for the specific ingredients listed (bacon, mushrooms, egg, tomato sauce, mozzarella). Serving size of 350g is estimated based on typical Italian restaurant individual pizza portion weight. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA component data and are not directly measured for this specific dish; these values are reported as best estimates and should be treated with caution. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as all fields have been estimated from approved USDA component sources. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input record appears to be a field survey platform tagging error and has been disregarded, as the dish description contains no chicken. Pizzotto is not a Singaporean dish; therefore, mandatory Singaporean multilingual fields (Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not required under G6. Chinese names are provided as supplementary aliases for Singapore market context only.

improvement_actions_taken: Estimated nutrient values from USDA SR Legacy weighted composition modelling for pizza with meat and vegetables as closest analogue.
Assigned GI of 57 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry (regular-crust cheese pizza, Atkinson et al. 2008).
Calculated glycemic load per serving as (57 × 95.2) / 100 = 54.2 based on 350g serving.
Disregarded erroneous 'Chicken Dish' cuisine tag from field survey input.
Applied FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes with honest gap notes; SNOMED CT set to null with 'none' match type.
Provided Chinese simplified and traditional aliases for Singapore market context.
Confirmed Italian cuisine does not require Malay/Tamil/Hindi multilingual fields under G6.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are fully consistent.
All per_100g fields are numeric; no -1 sentinel values used as all fields estimated from approved sources.
Documented estimation basis and all caveats in notes field.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
